exemplar.title: To what extent has Netflix's inclusion of games affected its profitability status?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Netflix's inclusion of games affected its profitability status?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The key concept of âchangeâ is identified and linked to Netflixâs strategic pivot into gaming.
Descriptive statistics about membership and revenue demonstrate an attempt to ground the concept in organizational context.
weaknesses: No explicit definition of âchangeâ or other key concepts at the outset to frame the argument.
Analytical integration of the concept is sporadic rather than woven throughout the discussion.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student names and describes âchangeâ in relation to Netflixâs gaming push, the treatment remains largely descriptive. There is evidence of understandingâmultiple statistics are tied back to the idea of changeâbut the concept is not consistently defined or analytically applied. This places the work in the middle of the band, warranting a 3/5.

strengths: Four supporting documents are correctly formatted and fall within the required 3â5 range.
Sources are all clearly relevant to Netflixâs gaming strategy, providing a breadth of perspectives (industry outlets, financial press).
weaknesses: Depth of analysis for each source is limitedâthere is no rationale for why each was chosen beyond topic relevance.
The student does not explain how the various sources complement or contrast one another in depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets the numerical requirement and chooses credible, relevant sources. However, without explanations of each documentâs specific value or depth, the selection sits above basic but below fully in-depth. A score of 3/4 accurately reflects this.

strengths: Narrative evidence on Netflixâs history and interactive entertainment shows an awareness of theoretical framing needs.
Some quantitative data (growth figures, margin discussion) hint at potential tools usage.
weaknesses: No explicit business tools or theoretical frameworks (SWOT, Ansoff, NPV) are named or applied.
References to data remain descriptive; relevance to a research question is only implied.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With no clearly identified models or frameworks, and only incidental relevance of data to a research question, the work demonstrates limited selection and application of tools. This aligns with a Band 2 performance, meriting 2/4.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: A wide range of quantitative data (revenues, subscriber figures, download metrics) is collected and presented.
The narrative occasionally links data points (e.g., Sensor Tower download data to engagement), showing emerging analytical skill.
weaknesses: Most data presentation remains descriptiveâinterpretation and comparative evaluation are sparse.
Numerous visuals and tables lack commentary or integration into the central argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs use of data is substantive and there are moments of integrated analysis, but much of the content is descriptive. This sits above superficial Band 3 but below consistently thorough Band 5. A score of 4/5 reflects mostly effective analysis with some sustained integration.

strengths: A conclusion section exists and attempts to draw together discussion points.
The student reiterates some key findings, showing an awareness of the need for closure.
weaknesses: Conclusions are lifted largely from source material rather than derived from the studentâs own analysis.
No explicit research question is stated, so the link between evidence and conclusion remains vague.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presence of a conclusion demonstrates basic practice, but its shallow connection to the analysis and lack of an explicit answer to a research question warrant a midâlow performance. Therefore, 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Headings and page order provide a basic roadmap for the reader.
Chronological presentation of excerpts offers some clarity in following the narrative.
weaknesses: Logical flow and transitions between sections are weak.
The overall structure is more archival (document by document) than argumentative.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA shows an appropriate structure with headings, but coherence and argument-driven organization are limited. This corresponds to a solid Band 2 performance out of 2.

strengths: Titles and source attributions are consistently present.
Formatting of text, images, and tables is generally tidy and legible.
weaknesses: Key IA elements are missing (research question, word count, full referencing).
OCR errors and duplicated captions indicate lapses in proofreading.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Most presentation requirements are met (all required elements except a few important ones), and the work is legible and organized. The omissions and errors limit the performance to 2/2.

strandSelections.reasoning: Content is ordered chronologically by document extracts rather than by a logical argument; headings exist but there is little coherence, indicating only limited structure.

All documents are clearly about Netflixâs gaming push and contain numerical data, yet they are largely journalistic overviews with limited depth for rigorous business analysis. Relevance is sound but depth is modest.

Basic elements such as titles and source attributions appear, yet other required components of an IAâproper referencing, word count, research questionâare missing, so at least one required element is absent.
Four distinct documents (Gam3s.gg, The Ringer, Wall Street Journal, Nasdaq) are identifiable, satisfying the 3-5 range.
Because tools are absent, there is virtually no application to a research question. Any relevance is incidental rather than deliberate.

The student pulls selected quantitative figures (revenue, downloads, margins) from the articles, but usage is largely descriptive rather than analytical or comparative, indicating superficial selection and use.
Apart from quoting statistics, there is minimal attempt to interpret, integrate or evaluate the information; the analysis of the research problem remains limited.
The work never states an explicit research question, hence the link between findings and any question is, at best, superficial.
No explicit business tools (e.g. SWOT, Ansoff, investment-appraisal) are identified; the work relies purely on narrative evidence, indicating a very limited selection.
A short âConclusionâ paragraph (lifted largely from a source) is present but it is not demonstrably derived from the studentâs own analysis, so consistency with evidence is weak.

Multiple statistics about Netflix memberships, revenue and its gaming ambitions demonstrate a clear descriptive link to the organisation; however, very little analytical commentary ties those details to a business argument. This amounts to a basic description rather than a sustained analysis.

The student names a key concept only implicitly (the material hints at âchangeâ as Netflix diversifies into gaming) and offers brief factual statements, but there is no purposeful definition, exploration, or embedding of the concept within an argument. The work therefore shows basic knowledge without meaningful application.

annotations.comment: Although detailed, the paragraph remains descriptive. The student should critique the strategic fit of single-player titles versus market leaders.
This caption is duplicated from the previous pageâremove redundancy or consolidate the image discussion into one location.
The student correctly lists the first supporting document in proper bibliographic format, which establishes a clear starting point for evidence gathering.
While the list of documents meets the numerical requirement, the student should add a brief rationale explaining each sourceâs relevance and depth to justify their selection.
This figure is included without caption or commentary. The student should annotate the chartâs key trends and link them to the research question.

The paragraph lists revenue and membership figures descriptively but lacks analytical insightâthere is no comparison or interpretation of what a 15% growth and 25% margin imply for Netflixâs gaming strategy.
Consider defining and integrating the chosen key concept (e.g., âchangeâ) at the outset to frame the subsequent discussion of Netflixâs investments in gaming.
The image occupies an entire page without accompanying text. The student should add interpretation or remove redundant visuals.
This sentence fragment âengagement with its audience.â lacks context and appears disconnectedârevise or integrate it into a complete analytical point.
The second supporting document is clearly presented, but the student should discuss how Lewis Gordonâs article deepens or challenges the first sourceâs perspective.
The historical narrative is engaging but lacks reference to any business model or theoretical framework (e.g., Ansoff matrix) that could ground Netflixâs shift into gaming.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Extensive quoting here adds color but the student does not critically evaluate or link the quotes back to a central argument about Netflixâs strategy.
The opening descriptive paragraph on narrative fit is insightful but would benefit from explicit analytical connections to revenue or engagement metrics.
This detailed company description reads as background but lacks transitions or topic sentences, which weakens the overall structure and flow.
The student uses positive reviews effectively to illustrate gaming qualityâthis adds narrative strength to the analysis.
This historical context on Netflixâs third act is valuable; it shows how past transitions inform the current strategic pivot.
The narrative on interactive entertainment is compellingânext, quantitatively link this trend to Netflixâs margin or cash flow performance for fuller analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The definition of âtransmedia storytellingâ is accurate but remains isolatedâlink Jenkinsâ concept directly to Netflixâs gaming strategy to demonstrate integration of theory.
Loombeâs quote is meaningful but needs a sentence interpreting its strategic implicationsâfor example, how âcohesively connectedâ experiences drive subscriber retention.
The OCR artifact in the formula should be correctedâensure any LaTeX or inline math renders clearly without stray overlines or placeholders.
The WSJ article is listed but the student should briefly state why this document adds depth or contrast compared to the first two sources.
The description of the cancelled AAA game remains descriptive; the student should evaluate the financial risks and strategic trade-offs of pursuing high-end titles.
Consider discussing how the âNetflix Game Controllerâ app could affect cost structure and user engagement in financial terms.
The photograph of the GTA library lacks analytical commentary. The student should explain its significance to Netflixâs content strategy.
Link the reported subscriber increase of 5.07 million directly to the gaming divisionâs performance to strengthen the argument.
The heading âa year earlier.â is vagueârevise to clearly indicate the contentâs purpose or timeframe.
The opening paragraph lacks a clear problem statement or research questionâestablish this to guide the reader through the analysis.
Link the slowdown in download growth to revenue projections or user-base engagement models to increase analytical depth.
The image of 'Too Hot to Handle 2' needs integration; explain how in-house versus licensed titles influence Netflixâs strategic positioning.
Current discussions about monetization options are outlinedâbut the student should apply investment-appraisal tools (e.g., NPV) to evaluate each optionâs feasibility.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The financial ratios table is presented without interpretationâhighlight the most significant trends and discuss their strategic implications.
The narrative link between Sensor Tower download data and subscriber engagement is well drawn and adds context to the figures.
The student provides raw growth figures but could calculate and discuss the percentage change $$\frac{81.2-28.7}{28.7}\times100\%$$ to strengthen quantitative analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The final page is blankâinclude a conclusion that explicitly answers the research question and ties together key findings.
